title: Son heir; better than angels
summary_text: God spoke long ago prophets—now in Son heir all worlds; brightness glory express image substance upholding word power purification sins sat right Majesty on high—better than angels name inherited.
key_points: God, having in the past spoken to the fathers through the prophets at many times and in various ways.
has at the end of these days spoken to us by his Son.
His Son is the radiance of his glory, the very image of his substance.
when he had by himself purified us of our sins, sat down on the right hand of the Majesty on high.
having become so much better than the angels.
evidence_phrases: spoken to us by his Son
whom he appointed heir of all things
the radiance of his glory
sat down on the right hand
so much better than the angels

title: Captain of salvation; flesh and blood partook
summary_text: Lest drift—what heard—how escape if neglect great salvation? Jesus crowned glory suffering—author salvation perfect through sufferings; sanctifier sanctified one—all of one—for which cause not ashamed call brothers; destroyed him death power devil; faithful high priest.
key_points: how will we escape if we neglect so great a salvation?
the author of their salvation perfect through sufferings.
For both he who sanctifies and those who are sanctified are all of one.
Since then the children have shared in flesh and blood, he also himself in like manner partook of the same.
that through death he might bring to nothing him who had the power of death, that is, the devil.
evidence_phrases: how will we escape
so great a salvation
the author of their salvation
he also himself … partook of the same
that through death he might bring to nothing
a merciful and faithful high priest

title: Jesus greater than Moses; rest
summary_text: Consider Jesus apostle high priest—faithful as Moses in God’s house; Christ Son over house whose house we if hold confidence. Do not harden hearts as wilderness—failed enter rest because unbelief.
key_points: consider the Apostle and High Priest of our confession, Jesus.
he has been counted worthy of more glory than Moses.
Christ is faithful as a Son over his house; whose house we are.
Today if you will hear his voice, don’t harden your hearts.
They weren’t able to enter in because of unbelief.
evidence_phrases: Jesus … faithful to him who appointed him
more glory than Moses
we are his house
don’t harden your hearts
They weren’t able to enter in because of unbelief

title: Sabbath rest; word piercing; great high priest
summary_text: Rest remains—some enter; ceased own works as God. Word living active piercing; naked open to eyes him. Great high priest passed heavens Jesus Son God—sympathize tempted sinless—approach throne grace.
key_points: There remains therefore a Sabbath rest for the people of God.
For the word of God is living and active, and sharper than any two-edged sword.
There is no creature that is hidden from his sight.
We don’t have a high priest who can’t be touched with the feeling of our infirmities.
Let us therefore draw near with boldness to the throne of grace.
evidence_phrases: a Sabbath rest for the people of God
the word of God is living and active
all things are naked and laid open
tempted in all points like we are, yet without sin
draw near with boldness to the throne of grace

title: Priest after Melchizedek; milk and solid food
summary_text: High priest taken among men can deal gently; not self-glorying called God as Aaron. Christ Son day You are priest forever Melchizedek order—days flesh offered prayers tears learned obedience suffering perfected author eternal salvation. Milk vs solid food—unskilled word righteousness.
key_points: So also Christ didn’t glorify himself to be made a high priest.
You are a priest forever, after the order of Melchizedek.
Though he was a Son, yet learned obedience by the things which he suffered.
having been made perfect, he became to all of those who obey him the author of eternal salvation.
You have need again for someone to teach you the rudiments of the first principles.
evidence_phrases: Christ didn’t glorify himself
priest forever … Melchizedek
learned obedience by the things which he suffered
author of eternal salvation
everyone who lives on milk
solid food is for those who are full grown

title: Repentance impossible; hope anchor
summary_text: Leave elementary teaching—impossible renew again repentance crucifying Son—land drinking rain bears thorns burned; beloved better things salvation—God not unrighteous forget work love—hope anchor sure entering veil.
key_points: Therefore leaving the teaching of the first principles of Christ, let us press on to perfection.
it is impossible to renew them again to repentance.
For God is not unrighteous, so as to forget your work.
This hope we have as an anchor of the soul, a hope both sure and steadfast.
evidence_phrases: let us press on to perfection
impossible to renew them again to repentance
they crucify the Son of God for themselves again
God is not unrighteous
an anchor of the soul
entering into the inner place behind the veil

title: Melchizedek priesthood; better covenant
summary_text: Melchizedek king Salem priest Most High—blessed Abraham—Levi tithed in loins. Priesthood changed—law changed—Jesus guarantor better covenant—one not daily like those many priests; perfected forever sat down.
key_points: without father, without mother, without genealogy.
he whose genealogy is not counted from them has taken tithes from Abraham.
For the priesthood being changed, there is of necessity a change made also in the law.
he has become the collateral of a better covenant.
because he continues forever, has an unchangeable priesthood.
evidence_phrases: Melchizedek, king of Salem
without father, without mother
the priesthood being changed
a better covenant
he continues forever
he was able to save to the uttermost

title: Heavenly ministry; new covenant
summary_text: Sum: we have high priest seated right Majesty heavens minister true tabernacle Lord pitched not man. If earth would not be priest—copy shadow heavenly. New covenant—laws in hearts; sins remembered no more—old obsolete.
key_points: We have such a high priest, who sat down on the right hand of the throne of the Majesty in the heavens.
a minister of the sanctuary, and of the true tabernacle.
he is the mediator of a better covenant.
I will put my laws into their mind, I will also write them on their heart.
Now what is becoming obsolete and growing old is ready to vanish away.
evidence_phrases: sat down on the right hand
minister of the sanctuary
mediator of a better covenant
I will put my laws into their mind
ready to vanish away

title: Earthly vs heavenly sanctuary; once for all
summary_text: First covenant had ordinances earthly sanctuary—blood goats calves not perfect conscience. Christ entered greater more perfect tabernacle once for all ages obtained eternal redemption—offered himself without spot through eternal Spirit—appear second time salvation.
key_points: Now indeed even the first covenant had ordinances of divine service, and an earthly sanctuary.
Christ … entered in once for all into the Holy Place.
now to appear once at the end of the ages to put away sin by the sacrifice of himself.
so Christ also, having been offered once to bear the sins of many.
to those who eagerly wait for him will appear a second time, apart from sin, for salvation.
evidence_phrases: an earthly sanctuary
entered in once for all
through his own blood
once at the end of the ages
offered once to bear the sins of many
appear a second time … for salvation

title: One offering; draw near; faith endurance
summary_text: Law shadow—one sacrifice perfected forever sat down—no more offering sin. Boldness enter holiest Jesus blood; hold fast hope; provoke love good works; sin willfully no sacrifice left—vengeance; live faith—just shall live by faith.
key_points: For by one offering he has perfected forever those who are being sanctified.
having therefore, brothers, boldness to enter into the holy place by the blood of Jesus.
If we sin willfully after we have received the knowledge of the truth, there remains no more a sacrifice for sins.
But we are not of those who shrink back to destruction, but of those who have faith.
evidence_phrases: by one offering he has perfected forever
boldness to enter into the holy place
there remains no more a sacrifice for sins
It is a dreadful thing to fall into the hands of the living God
the just shall live by faith
we are not of those who shrink back

title: Faith’s examples
summary_text: Faith substance hoped evidence not seen—these all obtained a good testimony through faith. Abel Enoch Noah Abraham Sarah Isaac Jacob Joseph Moses Rahab Gideon etc.—not perfected without us.
key_points: Now faith is assurance of things hoped for, proof of things not seen.
By faith, we understand that the universe has been framed by the word of God.
By faith, Abel offered to God a more excellent sacrifice.
These all died in faith, not having received the promises.
God having provided some better thing concerning us, that apart from us they should not be made perfect.
evidence_phrases: faith is assurance of things hoped for
the universe has been framed by the word of God
By faith, Abel
they were persuaded of the promises
a better resurrection
apart from us they should not be made perfect

title: Jesus author finisher; kingdom unshakable
summary_text: Cloud witnesses lay aside sin run race looking Jesus author finisher faith—cross shame despised—God’s discipline sons; peace holiness; see Jesus mediator new covenant—blood speaketh better Abel. Serve God acceptably reverence—kingdom unshakable.
key_points: let us also lay aside every weight, and the sin which so easily entangles us.
looking to Jesus, the author and perfecter of faith.
Consider him who endured from sinners such hostility against himself.
whom the Lord loves, he disciplines.
Therefore, since we receive a Kingdom that can’t be shaken, let’s have grace.
evidence_phrases: lay aside every weight
Jesus, the author and perfecter of faith
who endured from sinners such hostility
whom the Lord loves, he disciplines
you have come to … Jesus, the mediator of a new covenant
a Kingdom that can’t be shaken

title: Sacrifice praise; outside camp; grace
summary_text: Continue brotherly love; entertain strangers; remember prisoners; marriage honorable. Content—God never leave; Jesus same; strange doctrines not; altar those serve tabernacle—go outside camp bearing reproach—continual sacrifice praise; obey leaders; grace with all.
key_points: Let brotherly love continue.
Be content with such things as you have, for he has said, ‘I will in no way leave you, neither will I in any way forsake you.’
Let’s therefore go out to him outside of the camp, bearing his reproach.
Through him, then, let us offer up a sacrifice of praise to God continually.
The grace of the Lord be with you all. Amen.
evidence_phrases: Let brotherly love continue
Be content with such things as you have
I will in no way leave you
go out to him outside of the camp
a sacrifice of praise to God continually
The grace of the Lord be with you all
